target: F64 NumPy-broadcast binary ops (add/sub/mul/div/max/min/... ) e.g. bias-add [N,D]+[D]

lever.description: Add broadcast_binary_f64 fast path to broadcast_binary_tensors: for F64/F64 operands, reuse the identical broadcast index math (flat_to_multi_into + broadcast_flat_index) but apply the passed float_op directly to the gathered f64 values, skipping the per-element binary_literal_op dispatch (promote_dtype + 2x literal_dtype + 2x literal_to_numeric_f64 + literal_from_numeric_f64). Works for every binary primitive since float_op carries the operation. Returns None (fall through) on any non-F64Bits gathered element.

baseline.note: fast-path guard temporarily removed; generic binary_literal_op broadcast path

after.tool: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'eval/add_broadcast_bias_1k_f64'

isomorphism_proof.ordering: Identical broadcast index math (flat_to_multi_into + broadcast_flat_index) and row-major flat output order.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: Applies the same float_op the generic path uses for F64 (binary_literal_op(F64,F64) = from_f64(float_op(...))); exact per-element.
isomorphism_proof.rng: No RNG state is read or written.
isomorphism_proof.errors: Only F64/F64 broadcasts take the fast path; other dtypes/malformed literals fall through to the generic binary_literal_op path.
isomorphism_proof.unit_test: fj-lax arithmetic::tests::broadcast_binary_f64_fast_path_bit_identical (row [3] and column [2,1] broadcasts, add/sub/mul/div, NaN/-0.0/+-inf); 997/997 fj-lax tests pass.
